mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in real retail sales (RSAFS) signals robust consumer spending, driving corporate earnings growth and leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: If the directional accuracy of RSAFS month-over-month changes predicting 20-day forward SPX returns is less than 55% over a 3-year rolling window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 4.5%, it indicates labor market slack that will reduce consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to negative equity returns.
failure_condition: If the average 20-day SPX return after UNRATE crosses above 4.5% is not significantly negative (p>0.05) compared to unconditional average, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Jointly high and rising interest rates (FEDFUNDS > 4.0%) with declining retail sales (RSAFS month-over-month drop > 1%) signal a tightening cycle that suppresses consumption and investment, producing negative forward returns over quarterly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Historical correlation between tight money and retail sales drop precedes recessions
failure_condition: If the condition occurs fewer than 10 times in 10 years or the average 60-day forward SPX return is positive when the condition is true, reject.

mechanism_narrative: In an early-cycle expansion regime characterized by falling unemployment and low interest rates, rising industrial production (INDPRO) reflects robust economic activity and leads to positive S&P 500 returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Early-cycle expansions historically see positive equity returns with rising production
failure_condition: If during periods when UNRATE<5% and FEDFUNDS<3% (early-cycle proxy), the Rank IC of INDPRO MoM changes predicting 20-day SPX returns is non-positive over any 5-year window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) falls below 3.0%, it signals an accommodative policy stance that compresses discount rates and supports equity valuations over the next week.
failure_condition: If 5-day SPX returns after FEDFUNDS < 3.0% are not on average positive with a t-stat > 2, or if the hit rate is below 60% over 30 observations, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects expanding employment, which supports disposable income and consumer spending, driving corporate earnings and positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: If the Spearman rank correlation between PAYEMS monthly changes and subsequent 60-day SPX returns is below 0.05 over a 10-year period, reject.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous occurrence of rising interest rates (FEDFUNDS MoM increase > 25bp) and rising unemployment (UNRATE MoM increase > 0.1pp) indicates a stagflationary environment that erodes corporate margins and equity valuations.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS MoM chg > 25bp and UNRATE MoM chg > 0.1pp
evidence_refs: fact:Historical episodes of rising rates and rising unemployment coincide with negative equity returns
failure_condition: If the average 20-day SPX return during joint trigger events is not negative with at least 95% confidence (one-sided t-test), or if fewer than 20 such events occur in the historical sample, reject.

mechanism_narrative: During a housing market expansion (rising HOUST), residential investment contributes to aggregate demand and employment, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over short horizons.
failure_condition: If in periods when HOUST is above its 12-month moving average (expansion proxy), the average 5-day SPX return is not significantly positive (p>0.10) or the signal is not consistent across cycles, reject.
